title: Asparagus Soup
ingredients: 1 cup minced leeks, whites only, well rinsed
instructions: Trim asparagus, cutting the pretty top tips about 1 1/2 inch in length.
Cut the woody stem pieces from each spear and reserve.
Cut the remaining tender stalks into 1/2 inch pieces.
In a medium pot, bring stock to boil.
Add the tough woody asparagus stems and cook at a simmer until tender, about 30 minutes.
Remove the stems with a slotted spoon and discard them, reserving the broth/stock.
Add the decorative asparagus tips to the stock and blanch for no more than 1 minute.
Remove the tips with a strainer and place in an ice water bath.
Drain the tips on paper towels and reserve for garnish.
In a medium stockpot, melt the butter and add shallots and leeks, cooking until tender, about 3-5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the chopped tender asparagus stalks, salt, pepper, and Cavendar's Greek seasoning.
Add the broth to the asparagus and cook until asparagus is tender, about 20 minutes.
Using an immersion blender, puree the soup until smooth.
Add lemon juice and adjust seasonings to taste.
Stir in cream and heat until warm.
Ladle soup into bowls and top with reserved asparagus tips and freshly grated Parmesan cheese.
notes: The woody asparagus stems flavor the broth but are discarded after cooking.
Blanching the asparagus tips briefly preserves their color and texture for garnish.
An immersion blender creates a smooth, creamy texture without requiring straining.
